Platform & Data.narrative: This family ensures your field service system is secure, accessible, and connected to your other business tools. Singapore SMEs must comply with PDPA data protection rules, and your technicians need to work reliably on phones and tablets in the field. When your dispatcher integrates customer data from your accounting system or exports job records for invoicing, this family makes it seamless and safe.

Work Order Core.narrative: This is the heartbeat of field service—creating jobs, sending technicians to fix them, and capturing proof of completion. SMEs need this to turn customer requests into organised work, track what's been done, and prove compliance (especially for regulated services like HVAC or electrical work). When a technician arrives on-site, completes a checklist, takes photos, and gets customer sign-off, this family makes it all happen.

Scheduling & Dispatch.narrative: This family gets the right technician to the right job at the right time, saving fuel, reducing travel time, and keeping customers happy. SMEs with multiple technicians or wide service areas benefit hugely—you can see who is available, match them to jobs by skill and location, and track their movements in real time. When you need to reschedule a job because a technician is stuck in traffic, or optimise tomorrow's route to save petrol costs, this family delivers.

Equipment & Inventory.narrative: This family tracks what you service (customer equipment and installed bases) and what you carry (spare parts in vans and warehouses). SMEs need this to know whether a part is in stock before dispatching a technician, schedule preventive maintenance before equipment breaks, and avoid costly emergency calls. When you can tell a customer 'your air-con is due for servicing next month' or confirm 'we have the pump in stock', you look professional and reduce downtime.

Service Contracts.narrative: This family manages what you promise to customers (SLAs, warranties, coverage) and how you bill them. SMEs rely on this to know whether a repair is covered under warranty before dispatching, quote jobs accurately, and invoice automatically from completed work. When a customer calls with a breakdown, you instantly see their contract terms, response time obligation, and what they've already paid—and you can send them a professional invoice the same day.

Analytics & Intelligence.narrative: This family turns your field service data into actionable insights—how many jobs your technicians complete on the first visit, how long repairs take, and whether each job makes money. SMEs use this to spot bottlenecks (e.g. technicians spending too long on paperwork), improve technician productivity, and understand which service lines are profitable. When you see that your first-time fix rate is 75% and your competitor's is 90%, you know where to invest in training or tools.
